title: Asemeia tobatiensis
taxonomy: Asemeia tobatiensis is classified within the family Malpighiaceae. This family consists of about 133 genera of plants, commonly known as malpighiads. The genus Asemeia is part of the tribe Malpighieae, which is one of the main tribes within the Malpighiaceae family. The classification of Asemeia tobatiensis is further divided into species based on its unique characteristics and features. As a member of the Malpighiaceae family, Asemeia tobatiensis is a part of the order Malpighiales.

intro: Asemeia tobatiensis, also known as Tobati's asemeia or Tobati's false buttonbush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Asemeia tobatiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
